content.verdict: Sol y Luna Lodge & Spa is the Sacred Valley's most romantic — and most quietly purposeful — address. A Relais & Châteaux property tucked among 11 acres of flowering gardens just outside Urubamba (2870m, easier on the lungs than Cusco), it gathers 43 free-standing hand-painted casitas around fountains, lawns, and a heated outdoor pool framed by the snow-capped Chicón peak. Each casita is a small artwork — locally made furniture, hand-painted murals from Andean artists, fireplaces, and private patios — and the whole property exists to fund the on-site Sol y Luna school for valley children, so every cocktail you sip and every massage you book directly supports rural Peruvian education. The Wayra restaurant hosts a sunset Peruvian Paso horse show before dinner, the Yacu Wasi spa specializes in coca-leaf and andean-clay rituals, and the equestrian center offers horseback exploration of nearby Inca ruins. For honeymooners who want their week to feel meaningful as well as beautiful, this is the most distinctive lodge in the valley — and a near-perfect altitude-buffer between Cusco and Machu Picchu.
content.best_room: Book a Premium Casita with private terrace and fireplace — these standalone cottages have the best garden positions, soaking tubs, and the hand-painted ceilings the property is famous for. Honeymooners with extra budget should request the Romantic Suite or a Casita Suite with mountain view, where private outdoor spaces face the Chicón peak. Avoid the smallest Superior rooms if a fireplace matters to you.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lima arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land in Lima, overnight at a Miraflores or Barranco hotel. Light dinner of ceviche and pisco sours, early night before Cusco flight.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Fly to Cusco, descend to Sacred Valley
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning flight to Cusco. Skip the city overnight and drive 90 minutes down to the Sacred Valley (2870m, easier altitude). Check in to Sol y Luna, slow afternoon by the pool with coca tea.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pisac market and ruins
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive to Pisac for the artisans' market and the cliffside Inca ruins above the village. Lunch in Pisac, afternoon back at Sol y Luna, evening Wayra Peruvian Paso show before dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ollantaytambo and chocolate
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit Ollantaytambo's terraced fortress and living Inca town. Lunch at a hacienda, afternoon at the Choco Museo for a couples chocolate-making workshop. Couples massage at Yacu Wasi spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Vistadome train to Aguas Calientes
itinerary_7_nights.description: Train from Ollantaytambo down to Aguas Calientes. Bus up to Machu Picchu, full afternoon at the citadel. Overnight at Sanctuary Lodge or Inkaterra.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunrise at Machu Picchu, return to Sol y Luna
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn return to the ruins. Late-morning train back to Ollantaytambo, transfer to Sol y Luna for a final candlelit dinner in the gardens.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Horseback morning, depart
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final ride at the equestrian center on a Peruvian Paso through the valley. Late breakfast, transfer to Cusco airport, Lima connection for international departure.

caveats: The Sacred Valley sits at 2870m — gentler than Cusco's 3400m but still high. Hydrate, drink coca tea, and ease into activity on day one.
The Wayra horse show happens on set evenings — confirm the schedule when booking if it's a priority.
Avoid February and March — wet season, slippery ruins, Inca Trail closed in February.
Casitas are spread across the property and reached by garden paths — bring comfortable shoes and a flashlight for evening walks back from dinner.

faqs.question: Is Sol y Luna good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Outstanding — the standalone hand-painted casitas with fireplaces, the gardens, the spa, and the Peruvian Paso horse show before dinner make this one of the most romantic stays in Peru, with a meaningful charity model layered in.

faqs.question: Will we get altitude sickness?
faqs.answer: Less likely than in Cusco — the Sacred Valley sits 500m lower, and most guests feel fine after a slow first afternoon. Hydrate, sip coca tea, skip alcohol on day one.

faqs.question: How does it pair with Cusco and Machu Picchu?
faqs.answer: Many honeymooners do 1 night Cusco, 3 nights Sol y Luna, 1 night at Machu Picchu, 1 final night back at Sol y Luna or in Cusco. The valley's lower altitude makes it a much easier base than the city itself.

faqs.question: What is the school next door?
faqs.answer: Colegio Sol y Luna is a private school the hotel funds for valley children — guests can request a visit during the academic year and learn how their stay supports rural education.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May to September is dry and clear; April and October are quieter shoulder months. Avoid February and March wet season.

faqs.question: Are horse rides included?
faqs.answer: The Wayra dinner show is included in the rate. Horseback rides at the equestrian center are extra and can be booked at any level — beginner through advanced.
